commit.message:
fix: use IPv6 /64 prefix for stable visitor hashing

IPv6 privacy extensions rotate the interface identifier (last 64 bits)
regularly, causing the same user to generate multiple visitor hashes
throughout the day. Now only the /64 prefix (first 4 groups) is used
for IPv6 addresses, matching how Plausible handles this.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix critical PWA data loss bug: prevent sync errors from marking as complete

This fix resolves a critical issue where pro users were losing all data on PWA, particularly on mobile devices with flaky network connections.

Root cause: When cloud sync failed with a network error, the system incorrectly marked sync as "done", causing users to be forced through onboarding which overwrote their existing data.

Changes:
- sync-context: Only mark initialSyncDone=true on successful sync, not on error
- sync-context: Add 30-second safety timeout to prevent infinite loading
- sync-engine: Add exponential backoff retry logic (1s, 2s, 4s) for network failures
- OnboardingSimplified: Add protection to skip onboarding if user already has data

Impact: PWA will now properly retry failed syncs instead of showing empty state, preventing accidental data overwrites through onboarding flow.

commit.message:
perf: one-hot Dory commits with homomorphic hint combining (#16)

* perf: one-hot batch affine additions for Dory commits

Replace dense K×T field element expansion + Pippenger MSM for one-hot
polynomials (38 of 40 committed polys) with sparse index arrays + batch
affine point additions using Montgomery's batch inversion trick.

- Add batchInversion to MontgomeryField (1 inversion + 3(n-1) muls)
- Add batch_affine.zig with batchG1Additions/batchG1AdditionsMulti
- Add commitOneHotWithPool to Dory (direct G1 basis point summing)
- Store onehot_indices ([][]?u8) instead of dense witness_polys for
  sparse polynomials, reducing memory from ~1.2GB to ~2.4MB
- Sparse joint poly construction: O(T) per poly instead of O(K×T)

All 8 programs verified against upstream Jolt verifier.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf: use projective accumulation instead of naive affine adds

Replace batchG1Additions (1 field inversion per add) with projective
accumulation via addAffine (no inversions) + single toAffine at end.
For rows with ~64 points this eliminates ~63 expensive Fermat inversions.

primes_large: 33.8s → 17.4s (was 26.1s before one-hot change)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf: homomorphic hint combining for Stage 8 Dory opening

Cache G1 row commitments from the initial Dory commit phase and combine
them homomorphically (Σ γ^k · rows_k[i]) using GLV scalar multiplication
instead of recomputing row commitments from scratch via full MSM in Stage 8.

primes_large: 26.1s → 15.7s (40% total speedup)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: replace batch affine with projective accumulation, extract Dory row index building

Remove batch_affine.zig and switch one-hot Dory commits to projective
accumulation. Extract buildOneHotRowIndexSlices for reuse, add
combineRowCommitmentHints for homomorphic hint combining, simplify
one-hot index assertions, add Fp12.isOne(), and add tests for batch
inversion and one-hot commit correctness.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>


---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(insight-blocks): readPlatformMetrics from raw source tables (COUNT-based growth)

tech_metrics_cache.growth_ratio is always NULL (only 1 snapshot per source).
Rewrite readPlatformMetrics to query raw source tables and compute growth from
30d/60d time-windowed counts with Laplace smoothing. Each source query runs
independently so a missing table silently excludes that source.

Sources: npm_stats, pypi_stats, github_trending, arxiv_ai_papers, jobs,
         hackernews_stories, reddit_tech_posts, stackoverflow_trends

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: Add Gemini Antigravity agent documentation

This adds the agent configuration and setup files generated by the Antigravity assistant.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(browser-use): add payment context binding, origin allowlist, and prompt injection scanning (#68)

Browser agents making payments were not bound to origin, session state, or
action descriptions — allowing prompt injections on a page to potentially
smuggle transactions through the tool interface.

This adds three security layers to sardis-browser-use:

1. BrowserPaymentContext: captures origin, page_title, merchant, amount,
   purpose, and session_id at call time, then SHA-256 hashes them into an
   action_hash that travels with the payment to the ledger.

2. Origin allowlist: register_sardis_actions() accepts allowed_origins to
   restrict which page origins may trigger payments.

3. Prompt injection scanning: all string parameters (merchant, purpose,
   origin, page_title) are scanned against the same injection patterns
   used in sardis-api before any payment call reaches the SDK.

Each controller registration gets a unique session_id preventing
cross-session replay.

https://claude.ai/code/session_017ZDeyVY6Ai9ZzQ6fuuUSG7

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

board game has ~120 demand + 20 event cards (~14% event rate). This
change matches the real game's ratio.

SEO, performance en accessibility optimalisaties

SEO:
- Duplicate JSON-LD verwijderd van homepage (WebSite, Organization, Store
  staan al in root layout — Google zag dubbele schemas)

- Store addressLocality: "Nederland" → "Enter" (correcte plaats)
- Product OG type: article → weggelaten (beter voor e-commerce)

- Duplicate manifest link verwijderd uit layout head
- manifest.json: beschrijving geüpdatet (310+ was onjuist)

Performance:
- body::before will-change:transform verwijderd (niet-animerend element,
  veroorzaakte onnodige GPU layer promotie)
- Unused imports opgeschoond (getAllProducts, getEffectivePrice, REVIEW_COUNT)

Accessibility:
- Footer contrast: text-slate-600 → text-slate-500 (WCAG AA compliance)
- aria-hidden="true" op decoratieve SVGs in footer (sterren, hart)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resilient embedding job — guaranteed self-chain, throttle handling, per-product recovery

Root causes of stale jobs identified and fixed:

1. getTextEmbedding() now retries on ThrottlingException with backoff
   and returns null instead of throwing (prevents batch-killing errors)
2. computeEmbeddings() uses Promise.allSettled — one product failure
   no longer crashes entire batch; failed products auto-retry next batch
3. Self-chain is GUARANTEED via inner try/catch around loop — after()
   fires even after errors (except user stop/pause or job completion)
4. Pipeline fetchNextBatch wrapped in try/catch inside Promise.all
5. Self-chain fetch has 15s AbortController timeout
6. EMBEDDING_BATCH_SIZE: 40→20 (80→40 concurrent Bedrock calls,
   staying within AWS 50 TPS limit)
7. Batch sizes: 40→20 (images), 60→40 (text-only)
8. Heartbeat TTL: 90s→180s (synced in stats endpoint)

commit.message:
fix(updater): use .default for ESM-only update-notifier package

update-notifier v7+ is ESM-only. require() returns { __esModule, default }
so calling it directly as a function throws TypeError, silently caught,
leaving notifier=null and update checks never running.

fix: soporte multiplataforma para instalador Mac y Windows

  con hashes que rompe el empaquetado en macOS)
- schema.prisma: agrega binaryTargets darwin y darwin-arm64 para que
  Prisma incluya binarios de Mac (Intel y Apple Silicon)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: link C++ runtime for Tesseract static linking on all targets

Tesseract is a C++ library with a C API (capi.h). When FFmpeg's
configure tests linking via the C compiler, C++ symbols from
libtesseract.a are unresolved unless the C++ runtime is explicitly
linked. Add -lstdc++ (Linux/mingw) or -lc++ (macOS/llvm-mingw)
to extra-ldflags on all platforms.

commit.message:
feat: download monitor stuck-complete detection + force recheck

Torrents at >=99% with 0 seeds can have stale piece maps. Instead of
purging immediately, force a recheck first and track the hash. If still
stuck next cycle, then purge. Prevents removing torrents that just
need a piece map refresh.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: migrate Overseerr to Seerr v3.1.0 + Tdarr ulimits

- Replace linuxserver/overseerr with ghcr.io/seerr-team/seerr
- Update volume mount path (/config -> /app/config)
- Add init: true for proper signal handling
- Bump start_period to 60s for Seerr's slower startup
- Add ulimits (nofile 65536/524288) to Tdarr node for large libraries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: autoheal sidecar for VPN-dependent download clients

Watchtower updates to Gluetun break qBit/SABnzbd because they share
network_mode: "service:gluetun" and Watchtower doesn't respect
depends_on ordering on recreation. Download clients come up before
the tunnel is established, lose connectivity, and sit dead.

- Add willfarrell/autoheal sidecar to monitor labeled containers
- Label qBit and SABnzbd with autoheal=true
- Change health checks to test tunnel connectivity (ping 1.1.1.1)
  in addition to web UI, so a dead tunnel marks containers unhealthy
- Bump start_period to 90s and timeout to 15s to account for tunnel
  establishment time
- Autoheal checks every 60s, restarts unhealthy containers automatically

fix: use Node 24 and remove registry-url for OIDC publishing

Fix AUTH_SECRET: add to Dockerfile build args and DigitalOcean spec

Auth.js v5 requires AUTH_SECRET env var at both build time and runtime.
The deployed app was missing AUTH_SECRET entirely, causing /api/auth/session
to return 500 ("problem with server configuration"). Added ARG/ENV directives
in the builder stage so Next.js can compile auth routes, and updated the
DigitalOcean app spec to include AUTH_SECRET with RUN_AND_BUILD_TIME scope.

fix: correct http status attribute references in MCP logs search (#1814) ## Summary Fixes incorrect attribute path references in the MCP logs search filter bar and client-side trace grouping: - **Placeholder text** used `http.status` but the actual OTel attribute is `http.response.status_code` — users would type the placeholder example, get no autocomplete match, and think the feature was broken - **Client-side status extraction** used `http.status_code` instead of `http.response.status_code`, so the `StatusBadge` in trace rows likely never rendered HTTP status codes in the attribute-filtered view ## Test plan - [x] Open MCP logs page, verify placeholder now shows `http.response.status_code != 200` - [x] Type `http.response` in filter bar — autocomplete should show `http.response.status_code` - [x] Apply a `http.response.status_code != 200` filter — verify results filter correctly - [x] Check that trace rows display HTTP status badges when status code data is present Closes AGE-1521 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code) --- --------- Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6

commit.message:
Phase 3: Add clip generation pipeline with highlight detection

New: highlights.js — scoring engine (audio energy 40%, transcript
sentiment 30%, game keywords 20%, pacing 10%). Detects ~30 highlight
segments per 30-min recording, groups into 15-60s clips.

Pipeline handler (pipeline:generateClips) orchestrates: probe source →
create project → extract audio → Whisper transcribe → analyze loudness →
detect highlights → cut clips → thumbnails → save project JSON.

RecordingsView: wired Generate Clips button with real pipeline calls,
progress overlay showing stage/percentage, disabled state during generation.
App.js: loads projects from disk via projectList(), refreshes on generation.

commit.message:
Fix bullet truncation: disable thinking tokens for STI statement generation

Gemini 2.5 Flash uses thinking by default; without thinking_budget=0 the
thinking pass consumed most of the 4096-token budget, leaving <100 tokens
for the actual bullet text and causing mid-sentence cut-offs.

A single resume bullet needs no chain-of-thought — thinking_budget=0
disables it entirely and 1024 output tokens is ample for 1-2 sentences.

commit.message:
Fix SMT-LIB bitvector ops (sdiv, srem, udiv, urem, ashr, lshr) and add Kani proofs

- Fix sdiv/srem to use truncated division toward zero (not Python floor division)
- Fix udiv/urem to return SMT-LIB defined values on division by zero
- Fix ashr to properly sign-extend and use modular shift amounts
- Add generic lshr_fn/ashr_fn factories for arbitrary bit widths
- Add 40 Kani bounded model checking harnesses for Rust bitvector ops
- Add Python cross-validation tests (exhaustive 8-bit, Hypothesis property-based)
- Add Crosshair symbolic contracts for bitvector operations
